'''Shayne McMenemy'''  (born 19 July 1975) is a former [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a back rower in the 1990s and 2000s. He played club football in Australia for the [[Western Suburbs Magpies]] and [[WA Reds]], and in England for the [[Rochdale Hornets]], [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] and [[Hull FC]].

==Early years==
McMenemy was born in [[Sydney]], New South Wales, Australia. He attended [[Macquarie Fields High School]] in his early years, before attending [[St Gregory's College, Campbelltown]]. {{Citation needed|date=April 2017}}

==Playing career==
===Australia===
{{BLP unreferenced section|date=May 2022}}
A Macquarie Fields Hawks Junior, McMenemy made his first grade début in 1997 against the [[Balmain Tigers]]. The Magpies won the game against a Balmain Tigers team including [[Ellery Hanley]] and [[Paul Sironen]].

McMenemy signed a scholarship at 14 and went on to play Harold Mathews, SG Ball, Jersey Flegg, Presidents Cup, Reserve Grade and first grade. McMenemy début in the [[Tommy Raudonikis]] era in the late 1990s. McMenemy mostly played {{rlp|sr}} for the Magpies. A bad elbow injury finished off his career in 1999 and he joined the [[Super League]].

===England===
McMenemy's English career started at [[Rochdale Hornets]] where he played a handful of games before moving onto [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]]. McMenemy was named Player of the Year and Clubman of the Year, and was selected in the Northern Ford Dream Team of the year. [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] finished one game short of the grand final.

McMenemy signed on for [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] for the 2001 season but only played one game before he transferred to [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]. McMenemy was named player of the year in 2001 and Defensive player of the Year in 2002.

By mid-2003, Halifax were experiencing financial problems and McMenemy was signed by [[Hull FC]] midseason. Making his début against the [[Widnes Vikings]], McMenemy dislocated his elbow and only appeared in a few games at the end of the season.

McMenemy played {{rlp|sr}} for [[Hull FC]], and was part of the winning [[2005 Challenge Cup]] team at the [[Millennium Stadium]], [[Cardiff]] against [[Leeds Rhinos]], 25–24. McMenemy played a major role in the semi-final against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], scoring two tries in the 34–8.<ref>{{cite news|title=Hull humble holders|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2005/jul/31/rugbyleague.sthelens|newspaper= [[The Guardian]]|date=31 July 2005}}</ref>

Hull FC reached the [[2006 Super League Grand final]] against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], and McMenemy played at second-row forward in his side's 4–26 loss at [[Old Trafford (football ground)|Old Trafford]], [[Manchester]].<ref>{{cite news|title=St Helens 26-4 Hull FC|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/6035553.stm|accessdate=18 August 2013|publisher= [[BBC News]]|date=14 October 2006}}</ref>

McMenemy's career with Hull ended with a serious injury, when he dislocated his shoulder scoring against [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] at Belle Vue, requiring season-ending surgery.

He was named in the [[Ireland national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Training Squad|Ireland training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Ireland Name World Cup 40 Man Training Squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.rli.ie/story.php?id=53|title=Ireland Name World Cup 40 Man Training Squad|publisher=Rugby League Ireland|date=2008-08-01|accessdate=2008-08-02|archive-date=11 April 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200411004717/http://www.rli.ie/story.php?id=53|url-status=dead}}</ref> After not initially being selected for the final 24-man squad, he was called up as a replacement in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Ireland|Ireland]] squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Ireland suffer White injury blow">{{cite web|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7657596.stm|title=Ireland suffer White injury blow|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-09|accessdate=2008-10-10}}</ref>

In 2008 McMenemy returned to Australia to play in the [[Jim Beam Cup]] for [[WA Reds]] as captain-coach. He was included in the Jim Beam Cup NSW team in the Quad series.
